name: Cascadas de Covaleda – circular
region_intro: Hiking around Covaleda offers access to diverse natural features within the Soria province of Castilla y León, Spain. The region is characterized by dense pine forests, part of the "Comarca de Pinares," and is traversed by the nascent Duero River. It lies at the foot of the Sierra de Urbión, featuring mountain peaks and glacial lagoons, alongside numerous waterfalls. Best hiking trails around Covaleda The most popular hiking route is Cascadas de Covaleda – circular, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through forested areas to several waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puentes de Covaleda – circular by the Río Duero, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail follows the Duero River, passing historical bridges and offering riverside scenery. Local hikers also love the Piedra Andadera y Cueva del Tío Melitón – circular desde Covaleda, an 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Covaleda is defined by dense pine forests, the Duero River, and mountain landscapes with waterfalls and glacial lagoons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Covaleda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Covaleda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Covaleda?
A: Covaleda is characterized by its extensive pine forests, part of the largest in Europe, alongside the nascent Duero River. You'll also find impressive mountain scenery from the Sierra de Urbión, featuring peaks and glacial lagoons, as well as numerous waterfalls.

Q: Are there many waterfalls to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its many waterfalls. Popular routes often integrate these features, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057607362/cascadas-de-covaleda-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas de Covaleda – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through forested areas to several cascades. Other notable waterfalls include Cascada Río La Ojeda, La Chorrera, and Paso del Peñoncito.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Covaleda?
A: The autumn months are particularly beautiful, as the dense pine forests transform into a spectacle of vibrant yellows, golds, and oranges. Spring and summer also offer pleasant conditions for exploring, while winter can provide opportunities for ski touring, though some higher routes might be challenging due to snow.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, Covaleda offers routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10462886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso Peñoncito Waterfall – Ojeda River Waterfall loop from Paso de Frigidillas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6 km trail that takes about 1 hour 24 minutes, making it a good option for a shorter outing. The region also features the 'Sendero de los Abuelos del Bosque,' an easy 2-kilometer circular trail focusing on ancient pines.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Covaleda?
A: Many of the trails around Covaleda are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057607362/cascadas-de-covaleda-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas de Covaleda – circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062301310/puentes-de-covaleda-circular-by-the-rio-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes de Covaleda – circular by the Río Duero&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Duero River.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Covaleda?
A: The hiking routes in Covaleda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural features, including the dense pine forests, the numerous waterfalls, and the stunning mountain and river landscapes.

Q: Are there any significant landmarks or natural attractions to see near the trails?
A: Absolutely. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, one of the highest peaks in the Sierra de Urbión, or visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6224900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt;, a glacial lake. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural landmark in the area.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Covaleda area?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails available around Covaleda, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 16 moderate routes, and 9 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059317153/piedra-andadera-y-cueva-del-tio-meliton-circular-desde-covaleda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedra Andadera y Cueva del Tío Melitón – circular desde Covaleda&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.0 km trail that typically takes over 5 hours to complete, leading through varied and demanding terrain. Ascents to peaks like Pico Urbión also offer challenging experiences.

Q: Can I hike along the Duero River in Covaleda?
A: Yes, the nascent Duero River flows through Covaleda, offering beautiful riverside trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062301310/puentes-de-covaleda-circular-by-the-rio-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes de Covaleda – circular by the Río Duero&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that follows the river, passing historical bridges and showcasing the scenic riverside environment.

Q: What kind of forests will I encounter on the trails?
A: You will primarily hike through dense pine forests, which are a significant feature of the 'Comarca de Pinares' region. These forests are mainly composed of Scots pine, but you can also find black pines, beeches, oaks, junipers, and hollies, creating a rich and diverse woodland environment.
tips: The last of the 3 waterfalls on the Route of the Covaleda Waterfalls. The descent can be somewhat tricky and/or technical, so I recommend wearing the correct footwear and being careful when slipping. Once down we can see a beautiful waterfall that we will surely want to photograph.

The second of the 3 waterfalls is the Cascada de la Chorrera, as in the other 3, the descent can be somewhat complicated and/or technical, so I recommend wearing the correct footwear and being careful when slipping. Once down we can see a beautiful waterfall that we will surely want to photograph.
The Paso Peñoncito Waterfall is the first one we find going up but I recommend that we visit it last, since the most common route continues from the base of this waterfall.
